plan: The bug is lost QuerySet-level state: _iterable_class lives on the QuerySet, so pickling only queryset.query and assigning it to a fresh QuerySet leaves _iterable_class as ModelIterable even though the Query has values_select set, producing broken model instances instead of dicts. Fix in the QuerySet.query property setter in django/db/models/query.py: when the assigned Query has values_select truthy, set self._iterable_class = ValuesIterable before storing self._query = value. Single-file fix at the point where the pickled Query state is re-attached to a QuerySet; the compiler and iterable classes are correct consumers and need no change.
